items.title: Secretary Howard Lutnick is questioned by House lawmakers over his Epstein ties
items.summary: Lutnick said last year that he cut ties with Epstein, his former neighbor, in 2005. But the Epstein files indicate that the two kept in contact, including for a 2012 lunch on Epstein's private island.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship leaves Cape Verde after three evacuated
items.summary: A British man is among three evacuees sent to the Netherlands after displaying symptoms while aboard the MV Hondius.

items.title: Justice Department can keep 2020 ballots seized from Fulton County in Georgia, judge rules
items.summary: The Jan. 28 seizure by the FBI targeted the elections hub in Georgia's most populous county, which is heavily Democratic and includes most of the city of Atlanta.

items.title: What to know about potential US-Iran agreement
items.summary: The one-page memo calls for negotiations over 30 days.

items.title: MLB television ratings surge 44% through early part of the season ahead of potential lockout
items.summary: MLB ratings have surged 44% this season, the best start in nine years, as rule changes and pace of play improvements fuel baseball's impressive growth.

crypto.title: Crypto News

items.title: AI agents becoming more relevant than humans by 2035 has Big Tech 'terrified', says Hoskinson
items.summary: The founder and CEO of Cardano’s Input Output said Google, Amazon and Facebook are terrified because AI agents will not behave like humans when confronted with ads.

items.title: Iran war threatens jet fuel shortage that could disrupt summer travel in Asia and Europe
items.summary: Oil executives have warned that the supply disruption in the Middle East will soon cause actual fuel shortages in some parts of the world.

items.title: Hantavirus outbreaks rarely happen. This Andes strain is ‘a complicated public-health situation.’
